"space-persona-visibility-management", "ContentBody": "

Currently, space admins making changes to a Persona affects all assigned spaces, not just their own space.  

\n

As a space admin, I would like to only see/manage personas that are configured to impact only that specific space so that there is no confusion or security risk.

\n
To help troubleshoot/understand my Space, I would like to see how many Site Personas impact the space I am managing.  I should not be able to view the Site Persona names, only the count.
\n

 

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

"ContentHistoryID": 0, "TenantID": 1 }, { "ContentID": 109948, "EntityType": 44, "ContentTypeID": 44, "ContentTitle": "Space-Level User Impersonation for Troubleshooting and Page Setup", "ContentStub": "space-level-user-impersonation-for-troubleshooting-and-page-setup", "ContentBody": "

When you manage a Space, it’s hard to fix issues or set up pages if you can’t see the site exactly like your members do. Today, impersonation is limited to Site Admins, which doesn’t help Space Admins who are supporting their own teams.

\n

The idea is to allow Space Admins (and optionally Moderators) to impersonate users within their own Space. This would make it easier to diagnose role and permission problems, confirm whether content and widgets appear correctly, and preview page builder changes from the point of view of a Space Member or a custom role, all without involving a Site Admin.

\n

A few example scenarios:

\n
    \n
  • A member says they can’t see a page or widget; the Space Admin impersonates the member to confirm visibility and fix the permission.
    • \n
  • While building a new page, the Space Admin previews it as a Space Member to make sure the layout and content look right for that role.
    • \n
  • A custom role has special access; the Space Admin verifies exactly what that role can see to reduce back-and-forth.
    • \n
\n

This would extend the current impersonation concept to the Space level, limited to users in the Space, so Space Admins can support their communities more effectively while keeping broader site access restricted. Related use cases include role-based testing, new Space launches, and content audits. For reference on how impersonation works today, here’s the existing guidance: {{mention:97812:9}} 

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

When you manage a Space, it’s hard to fix issues or set up pages if you can’t see the site exactly like your members do. Today, impersonation is limited to Site Admins, which doesn’t help Space Admins who are supporting their own teams.

\n

The idea is to allow Space Admins (and optionally Moderators) to impersonate users within their own Space. This would make it easier to diagnose role and permission problems, confirm whether content and widgets appear correctly, and preview page builder changes from the point of view of a Space Member or a custom role, all without involving a Site Admin.

\n

A few example scenarios:

\n
    \n
  • A member says they can’t see a page or widget; the Space Admin impersonates the member to confirm visibility and fix the permission.
    • \n
  • While building a new page, the Space Admin previews it as a Space Member to make sure the layout and content look right for that role.
    • \n
  • A custom role has special access; the Space Admin verifies exactly what that role can see to reduce back-and-forth.
    • \n
\n

This would extend the current impersonation concept to the Space level, limited to users in the Space, so Space Admins can support their communities more effectively while keeping broader site access restricted. Related use cases include role-based testing, new Space launches, and content audits. For reference on how impersonation works today, here’s the existing guidance: {{mention:97812:9}}

"ContentBody": "

Idea submitted on behalf of {{mention:5401:15}} at Suburban Propane

\n

We are using polls and surveys as part of our Cybersecurity training and we offer prizes to employees who take the surveys each week.  However, there is no option to expire a poll or survey so often times employees will still click on the link after the time to take the survey has passed.  We attempted to just delete the survey so that employees could no longer take it, but it resulted in us losing all the data for that survey.  We would like the option to put an expiration date on the survey so it would no longer appear active after a certain date but we could still access the data from it on the backend.

\n

 

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

Idea submitted on behalf of {{mention:5401:15}} at Suburban Propane

\n

We are using polls and surveys as part of our Cybersecurity training and we offer prizes to employees who take the surveys each week.  However, there is no option to expire a poll or survey so often times employees will still click on the link after the time to take the survey has passed.  We attempted to just delete the survey so that employees could no longer take it, but it resulted in us losing all the data for that survey.  We would like the option to put an expiration date on the survey so it would no longer appear active after a certain date but we could still access the data from it on the backend.

\n

"add-complete-x-of-x-items-to-earn-the-badge", "ContentBody": "

Part of our challenge with our new intranet is getting users to complete the profiles.  But they will do stuff for badges.  When we launched, we had a challenge that included completing profiles.  For our second \"investigator\" challenge, we wanted to again include a step to add profile photo and completing the profile along with some other activities that weren't in the first challenge.  However, if we added the profile one, some people who did that for the first challenge wouldn't be able to earn the new badge.  So we had to leave it off.

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

Part of our challenge with our new intranet is getting users to complete the profiles.  But they will do stuff for badges.  When we launched, we had a challenge that included completing profiles.  For our second \"investigator\" challenge, we wanted to again include a step to add profile photo and completing the profile along with some other activities that weren't in the first challenge.  However, if we added the profile one, some people who did that for the first challenge wouldn't be able to earn the new badge.  So we had to leave it off.

"en-US", "OriginalCulture": "en-US", "IsAvaiable": false, "ContentHistoryID": 0, "TenantID": 1 }, { "ContentID": 109787, "EntityType": 44, "ContentTypeID": 44, "ContentTitle": "Personal CoPilot Chat History (Save Searches)", "ContentStub": "personal-copilot-chat-history-save-searches", "ContentBody": "

Since the CoPilot chat resets whenever a user navigates away from the screen, retaining past conversations would improve usability of the AI.

\n

This could be done by allowing users access to their own Analytics, or through a user-specific widget that stores and displays chat history based on permissions.

\n

Users could access their own analytics—such as the session summaries that are currently stored in the Analytics tab—without seeing data from others. Or, chats already saved in the system could be extracted and shown in a searchable format. One more option could to allow users to easily download chatbot content from the CoPilot window, thereby \"saving\" their chat history. 

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

Since the CoPilot chat resets whenever a user navigates away from the screen, retaining past conversations would improve usability of the AI.

\n

This could be done by allowing users access to their own Analytics, or through a user-specific widget that stores and displays chat history based on permissions.

\n

Users could access their own analytics—such as the session summaries that are currently stored in the Analytics tab—without seeing data from others. Or, chats already saved in the system could be extracted and shown in a searchable format. One more option could to allow users to easily download chatbot content from the CoPilot window, thereby \"saving\" their chat history. 

", "ContentSummary": "Users should be able to view their chat history with Axero CoPilot.", "AuthorID": 5457, "AuthorUserName": "samsol007", "UpdatedByUserID": 5533, "UpdatedByUserDisplayName": "ContentID": 109737, "EntityType": 44, "ContentTypeID": 44, "ContentTitle": "Album, Photo, and Video Sorting", "ContentStub": "album-photo-and-video-sorting", "ContentBody": "

The ability to completely customize the way photos and videos are sorted in the Photo and Video Libraries would be helpful for ensuring users see the most relevant content first, or to establish a logical flow if content gets uploaded out of order.

\n

This should also extend to albums, so that admins/users are capable of customizing what content users see at the top versus the bottom of the page.

\n

A drag and drop feature would be the most helpful for full customization. As of now, the sorting features on photos and videos leads to more work for admins to try to get the right configuration of elements so that content displays correctly. 

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

The ability to completely customize the way photos and videos are sorted in the Photo and Video Libraries would be helpful for ensuring users see the most relevant content first, or to establish a logical flow if content gets uploaded out of order.

\n

This should also extend to albums, so that admins/users are capable of customizing what content users see at the top versus the bottom of the page.

\n

A drag and drop feature would be the most helpful for full customization. As of now, the sorting features on photos and videos leads to more work for admins to try to get the right configuration of elements so that content displays correctly. 

", "ContentSummary": "A drag and drop sorting feature that allows admins and/or users to completely customize the order that albums, photos, and videos appear in respective libraries. "content-analytics-improvements", "ContentBody": "
    \n
  • It would be helpful to have a column that shows the date a piece of content was most recently updated, in addition to when it was published. That would make it easier to tell what content has not been updated in a while (we usually edit existing content instead of deleting and recreating, when possible).
    • \n
  • It would be nice if the content metrics included the pieces of content that got no views in the given range, instead of stopping at 1 view. That would make it easier to catch the content that no one is looking at, so that we know what might no longer be necessary to maintain. 
    • \n
  • It would also be nice to have the content ID included in the CSV download, so that we could more easily cross-reference between spreadsheets (as titles and spaces may change over time / we sometimes have multiple pieces of content with the same title).
    • \n
\n

Thanks!

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "
    \n
  • It would be helpful to have a column that shows the date a piece of content was most recently updated, in addition to when it was published. That would make it easier to tell what content has not been updated in a while (we usually edit existing content instead of deleting and recreating, when possible).
    • \n
  • It would be nice if the content metrics included the pieces of content that got no views in the given range, instead of stopping at 1 view. That would make it easier to catch the content that no one is looking at, so that we know what might no longer be necessary to maintain. 
    • \n
  • It would also be nice to have the content ID included in the CSV download, so that we could more easily cross-reference between spreadsheets (as titles and spaces may change over time / we sometimes have multiple pieces of content with the same title).
    • \n
\n

Thanks!

false, "ContentHistoryID": 0, "TenantID": 1 }, { "ContentID": 109704, "EntityType": 44, "ContentTypeID": 44, "ContentTitle": "Adding Persona Tab to Analytics", "ContentStub": "adding-persona-tab-to-analytics", "ContentBody": "

We would find it extremely useful to be able to see analytics by way of personas. We provision users automatically into some personas (other personas are one-offs), and it largely reflects their role in the organization. Since our organization has large sections of different job types, we assume that certain work groups are utilizing the intranet in various ways.

\n

Being able to confirm these assumptions, keep running analytics, analyze the data, and then make informed decisions would be helpful in how we approach enhancing user engagement.

\n

A thought might be to add a \"Personas\" tab next to \"Home,\" \"Users,\" \"Content,\" etc. on the Analytics home page. This would be easier for a Site Admin UX to navigate to a tab that houses the persona analytics (space engagement, content creation, views, logins, etc.) versus navigating through each one of those main tabs separately to then parse out personas and put them in another spreadsheet to get them all in one place.

\n

I am happy to expand on this if it's not making sense. 

\n

Thank you for the consideration!

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

We would find it extremely useful to be able to see analytics by way of personas. We provision users automatically into some personas (other personas are one-offs), and it largely reflects their role in the organization. Since our organization has large sections of different job types, we assume that certain work groups are utilizing the intranet in various ways.

\n

Being able to confirm these assumptions, keep running analytics, analyze the data, and then make informed decisions would be helpful in how we approach enhancing user engagement.

\n

A thought might be to add a \"Personas\" tab next to \"Home,\" \"Users,\" \"Content,\" etc. on the Analytics home page. This would be easier for a Site Admin UX to navigate to a tab that houses the persona analytics (space engagement, content creation, views, logins, etc.) versus navigating through each one of those main tabs separately to then parse out personas and put them in another spreadsheet to get them all in one place.

\n

I am happy to expand on this if it's not making sense. 

\n

Thank you for the consideration!

"file-folders-findable-by-search", "ContentBody": "

Hello!

\n

It would be helpful to have file folders findable in search. We often have users search general terms that apply to all the files within a folder (for instance, searching for the name of a template letter to download, when we have 5-7 versions of that template depending on the audience).

\n

Right now we can make a wiki to point to the file folder, but that is extra work to maintain and extra clicks for the end user. Often, a written description of the differences between the files on offer is not necessary (the title of the file provides enough clarity), so the wiki doesn't provide added value beyond search optimization.

\n

It would be ideal if we can opt in to making some file folders searchable, but not have every folder searchable by default (like the Page Builder page toggle), since we also have file folders that would gum up search results that are currently working well. Being able to promote the folder in search as well would also be appreciated.

\n

Thanks for your consideration!

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

Hello!

\n

It would be helpful to have file folders findable in search. We often have users search general terms that apply to all the files within a folder (for instance, searching for the name of a template letter to download, when we have 5-7 versions of that template depending on the audience).

\n

Right now we can make a wiki to point to the file folder, but that is extra work to maintain and extra clicks for the end user. Often, a written description of the differences between the files on offer is not necessary (the title of the file provides enough clarity), so the wiki doesn't provide added value beyond search optimization.

\n

It would be ideal if we can opt in to making some file folders searchable, but not have every folder searchable by default (like the Page Builder page toggle), since we also have file folders that would gum up search results that are currently working well. Being able to promote the folder in search as well would also be appreciated.

\n

Thanks for your consideration!

"ContentBody": "

I would like to see the Impersonate User feature available inside a space for Site Administrators. Currently, when testing functionality inside a space, admins need to leave the space, navigate to the control panel to locate and impersonate a user, and then return to the space. This process can be time-consuming and disrupt workflow.  It would be more efficient if the \"Impersonate User\" option was also available to Site Admins in the people list within the space.

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

I would like to see the Impersonate User feature available inside a space for Site Administrators. Currently, when testing functionality inside a space, admins need to leave the space, navigate to the control panel to locate and impersonate a user, and then return to the space. This process can be time-consuming and disrupt workflow.  It would be more efficient if the \"Impersonate User\" option was also available to Site Admins in the people list within the space.

response.name: REST API: Get Content List

query.description:
Type of entity to filter content by. Here are the values of Entity Type parameter:
Forum = 1
ForumGroup = 2
Article = 3
Blog = 4
Photo = 6
Video = 7
Wiki = 9
CMSPage = 12
File = 14
Album = 18
Idea = 44
ForumPost = 54
ForumTopic = 55
Comma-separated list of entity types to filter content by.
ID of the category to filter content by. Category ID for Articles, Blogs, Videos, CMS Pages & Ideas.
ID of the parent content to filter content by.
ID of the space to filter content by.
ID of the user to filter content by.

Status of the entity to filter content by. Here are the values of Entity Status parameter:
PendingWorkflow = 0
Published = 1
Draft = 2
Declined = 3
Updated = 4
Expired = 5
Spam = 6
All = 7
DelayedPublishing = 8
TemporarilySaved = 9
UnderProcessing = 10
Deleted = 11
Filter content by whether it is featured.
Filter content by the date it was published.
Name of the category to filter content by.
Comma-separated list of category names to filter content by.
Filter content by whether it is marked as an answer.
Filter content for the user's spaces.
Filter content by whether it is expired.

Column to sort the content by. Here are the values of Sort Column parameter:
DateCreated = 1
Rating = 2
DatePublished = 3
DateUpdated = 4
ViewCount = 5
CommentCount = 6
EventStartDate = 7
SortOrder = 8 (When SortOrder column is set for the content)
VoteCount = 9
ContentTitle = 10
DateAwarded = 11
TagCount = 12
MostRelavant = 13

Order to sort the content by. Here are the values of Sort Order parameter:
Ascending = 0
Descending = 1
Specifies the starting page of the list to be returned. (Default value is 1)
Specifies the number of results to be returned per page. (Default value is 30)
Logs the API call as an action in User Activity when set to true
Specifies the culture or language for retrieving localized content in the request URL. Example: es-ES (Spanish - Spain), en-US (English - United States).

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: October 31st, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

    • Reviewed newsletter demo prototype tasks. Most have been resolved, and I provided feedback on a couple of outstanding tasks
      • Task 39481: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Inline toolbar doesn't render sometimes
      • Task 39480: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Email Preview does not match the email that is designed
      • Task 39479: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Footer height in the preview does not match the height on the canvas
      • Task 39478: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Button placeholder text is hard to read and spills outside of the button
      • Task 39476: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Links in rich text editor are not styled correctly
      • Task 39468: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Excess height/width and padding when adding images
      • Task 39474: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Unable to see Block actions (move,copy,delete)
      • Task 39465: Newsletter - Demo Prototype - Editor is cramped at smaller screen resolutions
      • Task 39464: Newsletter dashboard layout breaking at 1700px - ensure fluid rendering at common breakpoints
    • Provided additional feedback on Task 38903: Design UI for Multiple Authors on Content
    • Attend CHG Healthcare Q3 site review
    • Made updates to pricing and early adopter offer in Newsletter demo documentation:
      • {{mention:109974:9}} 
      • {{mention:109939:9}} 
      • {{mention:109935:9}} 
      • Axero Newsletters Sell Sheet Deck
    • Revised and simplified the {{mention:109941:9}} documentation
    • Revised Newsletter demo templates, added talking points, and questions to help guide the conversation
      • {{mention:110027:9}} 
      • {{mention:110041:9}} 
    • Added Wiki {{mention:110115:9}} 
    • Scheduled Newsletter demo training for November 6th, 2025

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No challenges or roadblocks

Priorities for the next week:

  • Prepare for the Newsletters demo training
  • Provide training for demonstrating newsletters.
  • Work on grooming and specing user stories
  • Review ideas and follow up with customers
  • Create new user stories based on ideas
  •  Prioritize and ready Journey user stories for the upcoming sprint

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Will continue to work with Jennifer and Ahn Tran to deliver the newsletter training

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Nothing additional to add this week

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: October 17th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Finish slides for Newsletter training
  • Put together the suggested pricing structure for Newsletters
  • Work to get the Notify People bugs resolved
  • Had a very productive Analytics review with CSMs. We examined the Recognition analytics pages, discussed recognition in general, and explored the challenges our customers currently face with this feature, along with understanding trends in their users' engagement.
  • Q3 release demo
  • Created these user stories for the product planning backlog based on feedback/requirements from Modsquad
    • User Story 39263: Tags and Tag Groups - u.c. - Enable AI Tag Suggestions for Content
    • User Story 39265: Required Reading - u.c. - Enable Granular Assignment and Exclusions 
  • Created documentation and resources for the Newsletters training
    • {{mention:109937:9}} 
    • {{mention:109941:9}} 
    • {{mention:109973:9}}
    • {{mention:109938:9}} 
    • {{mention:109939:9}}  
    • {{mention:109935:9}} 
    • Newsletters Sell Sheet Deck
    • Newsletters Sell Sheet Document

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Anh Tran has been encountering bugs and difficulties with windsurf in the demo prototype we plan to use for newsletter training

Priorities for the next week:

  • Help identify and resolve bugs in the demo prototype
  • Communicate any updates/changes implemented in the demo prototype to dev to ensure the newsletter release matches
  • Revise and finalzie Newsletter training material
  • Review AEIS feature requests with Wendi and Theresa
  • PerformYard training
  • Map Application features to My.Axero Ideas: Create/update Ideas, align statuses with Q4/Q1 sheet, and provide solution details for active (In Review/Development) items

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Will work closely with Anh Tran to ensure demo prototype is ready for training
  • Will work with Jennifer to finalize pricing and other details for Newsletters

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • I've learned a lot about preparing documentation and training for upcoming major feature releases. It was a valuable experience to understand different pricing models and how they might affect our customers' adoption and use of a new feature.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: October 10th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Wrote acceptance criteria for User Story 38946: Newsletters - v1.0 - Throttling Settings and Background Job
  • Wrote description and acceptance criteria for User Story 38949: Newsletters - v1.0 - Add “Newsletter” Option to User Notifications
  • Provided feedback on Bug 35844: After adding big number in delay time, it is showing 0 after saving.
  • Received champion approval and added acceptance criteria for User Story 38551: Analytics - vU.C - Flexible Multi-Select Filters (Content Distribution)
  • Updated description and sought feedback for User Story 35981: Journeys - v1.1 - Replace Infinite Scroll With Standard Pagination for All Journey Table Views
  • Reviewed existing bugs related to the Notify People recipient selection component that we are reusing for Newsletters. This could potentially impact the timeline
    • L-Blockers 
      • Bug 36608: UI issue on the Notify People page.
      • Bug 30507: Internal Server Error (500) and Incorrect Matching People Count when adding recipients by Sitewide Roles in email newsletter.
      • Bug 30763: Inactive personas should be excluded from the persona dropdown in the email newsletter/notify people modal.
    • Not-Blocker
      • Bug 17236: Notify people via wall post does not show the entry on the Activity stream.
    • Needs Dev Review
      • Bug 1046: HTML code is not applied to the email notification sent to the user.
  • Worked on Newsletter training
  • Researched the pricing models of competitors for Newsletters
  • Met with Bryce for our Product/Sales sync
  • Reviewed Analytics with the CSM team

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No real challenges this week.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Finish slides for Newsletter training
  • Put together the suggested pricing structure for Newsletters
  • Work to get the Notify People bugs resolved
  • Analytics review with CSMs
  • Q3 release demo 

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • I'll continue to work with Dev to move Newsletters along and help with any blockers that come up, like the Notify People bugs
  • Continue working with Anh Tran on the Newsletter Demo
  • I'll need Jennifer's feedback on the Newsletter pricing 

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • We decided to reuse the recipient selection component of the Notify People feature in the upcoming Newsletter feature. It was recently brought up that there are existing bugs related to the recipient selection component that will also affect Newsletters. Fixing these bugs now could improve the adoption of Newsletters, but might negatively impact the timeline and delay the release. This consideration was not made when the decision was originally taken to reuse the component, and we should keep this scenario in mind when building new features in the future.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: October 3rd, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Newsletters
    • Readied the following user stories for this upcoming sprint, all stories have been approved and sized.
      • User Story 36381: Newsletters - v1.0 - Manager Dashboard - View, Search, Sort, and Manage Newsletters
      • User Story 37555: Newsletters - V1.0 - Access Newsletter Manager from Multiple Entry Points
      • User Story 36379: Newsletters - v1.0 - Select and Manage Newsletter Recipients
      • User Story 36380: Newsletters - v1.0 - Send, Schedule, and Test Newsletters
  • Worked on acceptance criteria for Site and Space Persona permissions
    • User Story 38555: Personas: Site-Level Permissions
    • User Story 38902: Space Persona Permissions & Visibility
  • Worked with Anh Tran to set up a demo newsletter prototype site for CSMs
  • Worked on newsletter training for CSMs, including example newsletters that can be built in the demo prototype
  • Compiled a list of user stories and ideas to consider for potential development in Q4 and quick wins
    • List of User Stories for Q4 and Quick Wins
  • Defined and scoped these user stories
    • User Story 38622: Tags/Tag Groups - u.c. - Intuitive tag/tag group creation with clear guidance and default behavior
    • User Story 38930: Journeys - vU.C. - Enable @Mentions in Custom Content Step
    • User Story 34406: Content Feedback System – V1.0 – Enable Private Feedback Submission
    • User Story 36860: Analytics Reporting - Aggregate Integrated File Activity with Source Filter

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • I found it challenging to track customer ideas, suggestions, cases, or feedback that contributed to user stories or to identify customers who would benefit from user stories to calculate an ROI for prioritization.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continue working on Newsletter training
  • Analytics review with CSMs
  • Product/Sales sync
  • Continued scoping of user stories for Q4 and beyond

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Will continue working with Anh Tran to ready the newsletter demo site
  • Will work with Jennifer on the newsletter training

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • I've continued to work on and improve my user stories so they are easier to understand and still include all relevant information

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: September 26th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week: 

  • Reviewed and provided feedback on these bugs
    • Bug 38137: Customer Case - In Journeys, Select All users is not working as expected
    • Bug 35844: After adding big number in delay time, it is showing 0 after saving.
  • Worked on Newsletter user stories for sending and selecting recipients
    • User Story 36379: Newsletters - v1.0 - Select and Manage Newsletter Recipients
    • User Story 36380: Newsletters - v1.0 - Send and Test Newsletters
    • User Story 36388: Newsletters - v1.0 - Schedule and Send Scheduled Newsletters
  • Worked on these user stories for Journeys and gathered feedback from CSMs
    •  User Story 36396: Journeys - vU.C - Enrollment Automation - Automate Member Addition to Journey
    • User Story 38157: Journeys – vU.C – Trigger-Based Auto Enrollment
    • User Story 38158: Journeys – vU.C – Self Enrollment
  • Simplified User Story 35595: Activity Emails - v1.1 - Do Not Send Empty Digest Emails (Daily and Weekly) based on feedback
  • Created User Story 38622: Tags/Tag Groups - u.c. - Intuitive tag/tag group creation with clear guidance and default behavior

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • The only challenge was the retreat happening this week and the communication being a bit slower than usual, but it wasn't a big problem and was understandable and expected.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Working on team training and a demo prototype for Newsletters
  • Wrap up Newsletter user stories for sending and selecting recipients
    • User Story 36379: Newsletters - v1.0 - Select and Manage Newsletter Recipients
    • User Story 36380: Newsletters - v1.0 - Send and Test Newsletters
    • User Story 36388: Newsletters - v1.0 - Schedule and Send Scheduled Newsletters
  •  Analytics review with the CSM team
  • Journeys feedback call with CSM/TAMs

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • I will be working with Jennifer on the Newsletter team training for the experience team
  • Will work with Anh Tran to get the Demo site for Newsletter deployed. We will also be polishing the experience up a little so CSM can demo Newsletters to customers.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • I'm excited to work on the team training. I'm glad we're moving to demo Newsletters to customers so soon, as I think it will keep the momentum going around Newsletters while it's in development.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: September 19th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Analytics review part 3: Content analytics
    • Reviewed Content Distribution analytics
    • Created User Story 38551: Analytics - vU.C - Flexible Multi-Select Filters (Content Distribution)
  • Worked on permissions for Personas to address vulnerabilities and concerns around their use in the Notify People feature
    • Met with {{mention:5533:15}}  
    • Created User Story 38555: Personas – V1.0 – Enable Permissions & Visibility for Non-Admin Discovery and Use in Notify People
    • Followed up with {{mention:3562:15}} for feedback and review
  • Worked on Newsletter user stories and consolidated several
    •  User Story 36379: Newsletters - v1.0 - Select and Manage Newsletter Recipients
    • User Story 36380: Newsletters - v1.0 - Send and Test Newsletters
    • User Story 36388: Newsletters - v1.0 - Schedule and Send Scheduled Newsletters
    • User Story 36381: Newsletters - v1.0 - Manager Dashboard - View, Search, Sort and Manage Newsletters
    • User Story 37555: Newsletters - V1.0 - Access Newsletter Manager from Multiple Entry Points
  • I was asked to provide details and acceptance criteria for User Story 35595: Activity Emails - v1.1 - Do Not Send Empty Digest Emails (Daily and Weekly) to address a bug where empty activity emails are sent to users. 
    • I defined and provided acceptance criteria for the user story and sought feedback from {{mention:3070:15}} and {{mention:2740:15}} 

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Nothing at the moment

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Complete Newsletter user stories for sending and selecting recipients
    • User Story 36379: Newsletters - v1.0 - Select and Manage Newsletter Recipients
    • User Story 36380: Newsletters - v1.0 - Send and Test Newsletters
    • User Story 36388: Newsletters - v1.0 - Schedule and Send Scheduled Newsletters
  • Work on these user stories for Journeys and gather feedback from CSMs
    •  User Story 36396: Journeys - vU.C - Enrollment Automation - Automate Member Addition to Journey
    • User Story 38157: Journeys – vU.C – Trigger-Based Auto Enrollment
    • User Story 38158: Journeys – vU.C – Self Enrollment
  • Continue scoping and writing acceptance criteria for User Story 38555: Personas – V1.0 – Enable Permissions & Visibility for Non-Admin Discovery and Use in Notify People
  • Simplify User Story 35595: Activity Emails - v1.1 - Do Not Send Empty Digest Emails (Daily and Weekly) based on feedback

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Continue working with champions and stakeholders to get sign-off on user stories

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Learning to find the balance for user stories that meet everyone's expectations and contain enough details and information, but not too much.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: September 12th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Readied User Story 36377: Newsletters - v1.0 - Design Newsletter with WYSIWYG Drag-and-Drop Editor for sprint 72/73
    • Received champion and architect approval
  • Discussed changes to the Text Block UI in the Newsletter Editor. Made updates to the appropriate user stories and prototype
    • created and updated tasks
      • Task 38108: Update Text Block to use standard TinyMCE editor
      • Task 38085: Add Design Note for Richt Text Editor, Mentions and Upload Image
  • Readied User Story 36384: Newsletters - V1.0 - Role-Based Permissions and Access Control for Sprint 72/73
  • Cleaned up and consolidated existing Newsletter user stories to clean up the product planning and web app backlogs
  • Analytics call part 2: met with the CSMs to continue our review of Analytics, this time we covered the Users analytics page
  • Attended the Sales and Product sync to learn what customers are looking for when considering digital workplace solutions.
    • Reviewed a form submission tool Bryce created
    • Discussed the desire to receive notifications before content expiration
    • A content calendar for managing and scheduling content publication
    • Overall content governance and health tools
  • Discussed Journeys APIs with Victoria and created the following user stories
    • User Story 38157: Journeys – vU.C – Trigger-Based Auto Enrollment
    • User Story 38158: Journeys – vU.C – Self Enrollment

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Aligning on the rich text editor design and scope for newsletters presented some challenges. To streamline going forward, I've got several Journeys user stories lined up and will be bringing in champion and architecture feedback much earlier in the process. This should help us tackle any concerns upfront, keep things agile, and make sure we're all aligned before diving deep into design and development.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Analytics review part 3: Content analytics
  • Work on Newsletter user stories
    •  User Story 36379: Newsletters - v1.0 - Select and Manage Newsletter Recipients
    • User Story 36380: Newsletters - v1.0 - Send and Test Newsletters
    • User Story 36388: Newsletters - v1.0 - Schedule and Send Scheduled Newsletters
    • User Story 36381: Newsletters - v1.0 - Simple UI for Viewing, Searching, and Managing Newsletters
    • User Story 37555: Newsletters - V1.0 - Accessing Newsletter Manager from My Account
  • Work on user stories for Journeys
    • User Story 36396: Journeys - vU.C - Enrollment Automation - Automate Member Addition to Journey
    • User Story 38157: Journeys – vU.C – Trigger-Based Auto Enrollment
    • User Story 38158: Journeys – vU.C – Self Enrollment

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Continue working with the Application Pod and specifically Anh Le as Newsletters progress. He will be providing updates as development continues, so we can quickly address any issues or changes that arise.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Always working to learn and improve!

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: August 29th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Reviewed these bug fixes, Journey navigation glitch, and Journey header misalignment, and Bug 36644: Customer Case - In Journeys, unable to deselect the "Require Step Completion Box" for Custom Content. These patches were rolled out to some of our customers on Journeys as of August 22nd. We had several that were updated on Sunday, August 24th. Luis let me know all customers on 65 should have the patches by September 11th. I will see if any customers would be blocked by not having the fixes sooner.
  • I reviewed User Story 36801: Prototype: Automate Journey Participant Enrollment and approved the updates. I asked some clarifying questions about how we handle duplicate enrollment. We will likely need to consider allowing users to take a journey multiple times. We have customers who want to be able to use Journeys for repeated processes, so a user would need to be able to complete the same journey multiple times. Right now, we have omitted the option to allow the user to re-enroll in a journey after a set timeframe since our current logic prevents users from retaking a journey they already completed.
  • Continued creating user stories for Newsletters. I'm working on refining this process so it's smoother and quicker to maintain in the future. I have found that you can bulk import user stories from a CSV file, which is a game-changer; however, I need to do some cleanup to the formatting for the acceptance criteria and descriptions. 
  • Meet with Luis and Theresa to go over how Limited releases are determined and managed. Learned to read and understand several dashboards and gained insight into the process for managing sprints across development and QA. This was a very useful call and really gave me a better understanding of the timing around our release cycles.
  • Had a call with Alex to discuss some of the copy used in the upcoming Newsletter feature and to learn about the messaging the company is using in our marketing. This is useful to keep the product aligned with the overall messaging and ensure we are all pulling in the same direction. 
  • Asked Theresa for some help reviewing some of the user stories I created for Newsletters. She offered some great insight and helped identify that several stories could be combined to make the user journey more apparent

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • This week, my biggest challenge was with the granular approach taken with the user stories for newsletters. There is a lot more detail in all the user stories, but several could be combined to create a more complete picture of the feature. 

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Wrap up the user stories for newsletters. I have a few that need to be consolidated and will also need champion review.
  • Meeting with Mariner Finance to learn more about their current challenges and to encourage adoption of Journeys
  • Scoping larger, more impactful features for journeys

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Continued collaboration with the other project managers as well as the dev team, as we continue working on Newsletters and Journeys

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Being walked through how Luis manages releases was very helpful. It's useful to know how other teams operate, and be able to understand the status and stages that each user story goes through before it's ready for release.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: August 22nd, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Resolved some questions and comments with Joan and Anh Le about the tech design for newsletters; we are now able to move forward with creating user stories for this feature. 
  • worked on and created 17 user stories for Newsletters, which encompass the creation and design of newsletters, along with the permissions needed to use newsletters. Created a permission matrix for newsletters to keep all user stories aligned and acceptance criteria role-based. This will allow the dev team to get newsletter user stories into the next sprint
  • I reviewed the transcripts and created summaries of five customer interviews I did for Newsletters. I used this information to create a spreadsheet to determine how these customers prioritize the features we deferred from Newsletters V0.0, so we can strategize on the fast follow-up features and identify any features we should be including in V0.0 to drive adoption. I added additional customers who have expressed interest in Newsletters to this sheet as well, so we can include them in this feature prioritization as we continue to have conversations with them
  • I reviewed all outstanding questions Anh Tran had regarding the Newsletter prototype. I added comments for a few adjustments and approved the merge requests.
  • I also reviewed and approved User Story 36801: Prototype: Automate Journey Participant Enrollment and provided some additional feedback.
  • I reviewed existing bugs for Journey v0.0 to identify any outstanding L-blockers. The navigation glitches and the inability to deselect require step completion for custom content type steps have been resolved, and patches appear to have been deployed as recently as Thursday. 
  • I also had calls with Peerage Realty and Earthjustice to discuss Journeys. Both seem ready to start testing Journeys with users; they just want to ensure some of the bugs we recently resolved are no longer an impediment. Lisa with Peerage was going to follow up with their other teams so they can move forward with updating their main site and start testing with users. 

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Right now, my challenge is including and addressing permissions, API, and database needs in acceptance criteria. I included what I thought was appropriate in the user stories I created for Newsletters, but I will likely need additional input on this from dev.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • I plan on creating the remaining user stories for Newsletters V0.0
  • I'll review any additional user stories for journeys and newsletters prototypes
  • Scope user stories for Journeys enhancements in product planning
  • Follow up with customers on Journeys patches and updates 

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Will need to work with Dev to ensure the acceptance criteria for newsletter user stories have all the details they need when it comes to API, Databases, and permissions. I outlined a permission matrix for newsletters so we can maintain consistency across user stories and ensure we do not miss any permissions or roles needed. This may not align with how they currently document permissions, so I will need to collaborate with the team to ensure this is formatted in a way that's easy to understand and doesn't deviate from any standards they may have in place. This will likely be the same for APIs and databases as well 
  • I'll continue working with the CSMs to ensure our customers move forward with rolling out Journeys to their end users. 

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • I utilized the new Product wikis that outline processes and expectations for user stories and found it helpful to ensure nothing was left unaddressed as I created the user stories for newsletter development.

  • I reviewed all outstanding questions Anh Tran had regarding the Newsletter prototype. I added comments for a few adjustments and approved the merge requests.
  • I also reviewed and approved User Story 36801: Prototype: Automate Journey Participant Enrollment and provided some additional feedback.
  • I reviewed existing bugs for Journey v0.0 to identify any outstanding L-blockers. The navigation glitches and the inability to deselect require step completion for custom content type steps have been resolved, and patches appear to have been deployed as recently as Thursday. 
  • I also had calls with Peerage Realty and Earthjustice to discuss Journeys. Both seem ready to start testing Journeys with users; they just want to ensure some of the bugs we recently resolved are no longer an impediment. Lisa with Peerage was going to follow up with their other teams so they can move forward with updating their main site and start testing with users. 

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Right now, my challenge is including and addressing permissions, API, and database needs in acceptance criteria. I included what I thought was appropriate in the user stories I created for Newsletters, but I will likely need additional input on this from dev.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • I plan on creating the remaining user stories for Newsletters V0.0
  • I'll review any additional user stories for journeys and newsletters prototypes
  • Scope user stories for Journeys enhancements in product planning
  • Follow up with customers on Journeys patches and updates 

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Will need to work with Dev to ensure the acceptance criteria for newsletter user stories have all the details they need when it comes to API, Databases, and permissions. I outlined a permission matrix for newsletters so we can maintain consistency across user stories and ensure we do not miss any permissions or roles needed. This may not align with how they currently document permissions, so I will need to collaborate with the team to ensure this is formatted in a way that's easy to understand and doesn't deviate from any standards they may have in place. This will likely be the same for APIs and databases as well 
  • I'll continue working with the CSMs to ensure our customers move forward with rolling out Journeys to their end users. 

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • I utilized the new Product wikis that outline processes and expectations for user stories and found it helpful to ensure nothing was left unaddressed as I created the user stories for newsletter development.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: August 15th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Met with First International Bank to discuss Journeys. They're also encountering a bug related to opening the side menu using the My Journey button in the header. We created a case, and I believe we already have a fix for this that will be rolled out shortly. They provided a lot of great insights and suggestions. We discussed upcoming enhancements to auto-enrolling users based on personas, roles, and spaces. They also suggested a use for trigger-based enrollment as well as self-enrollment into journeys. They raised some confusion they had around editing/pausing/stopping a journey, and highlighted the need to be able to clone an existing journey. They would like to be able to implement due dates or at least suggested timeframes for when a user should complete a step, as well as being able to include events as a content type. They also want to leverage the Journey API to customize and automate some aspects of journey management, as well as dive deeper into analytics. Overall, they seemed very happy with the feature and decided to leverage it over a custom solution they were working on, which used tasks and scripts.
  • Had a shorter call with Chloie from CHG to discuss some of her questions around journeys and gather additional feedback. She has not tested Journeys with any of her users yet, but is planning on some trials shortly. We discussed another call after they've had the chance to trial a Journey. She did call out the need to be able to more easily delete journeys and hide or separate archived journeys from all journeys to make management of journeys easier.
  • I worked with Theresa on a challenge Monterey Peninsula College encountered when attempting to notify users in a space of new content published in that space. This led to a few solutions and enhancements to current features that would allow them to achieve their goals. This also opened up a discussion around the Notify People feature, as well as the continued expansion of use cases for Personas, and how we may need to offer more options for visibility of personas for various roles and permissions outside of the admin/moderator role.
  • I also worked to address some of Dev's questions around Ghostwriting logic. I took time to test, observe, and document a variety of scenarios with one and several ghost writers to understand how our system handles the various scenarios and records and displays edits and updates to ghost-written content. I documented the various states and created a decision matrix defining the inputs and outputs so it's easier to identify the save state and displayed information given the various inputs. I'm hoping this will help Nhien with Bug 36871: Customer Case - Ghostwriting Attribution Overrides Author When Editing Wiki with MultipleGhostWriters Enabled, and may prove useful for our TSEs. I'll be sharing this with Maxwell next week.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • This bug Bug 37156: Customer Case - Notify People shows recipients and spaces without appropriate access, created a good conversation around permissions/visibility and security, and how we thread that needle to keep our product secure without limiting its flexibility and negatively impacting the user experience. This will likely generate several new user stories that I feel will be a bit challenging to define and scope due to the complicated nature of roles and permissions. 

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Continuing to work on Journeys, identifying and ensuring all bugs and user stories are correctly prioritized. We have around 22 customers currently on the beta release, and we need to get some more serious bugs patched before we continue rolling this out to new customers. I'll be working to identify the customers that have already received fixes and work to get patches scheduled for the other customers
  • Continued work on newsletters. I have several user stories and updates to the prototype to review. We had also discussed some possible frameworks that could be potentially used for newsletters. I reviewed some of the suggestions with Jennifer this week, and will likely gather some additional information on the various options this next week, so I can provide a better ROI on these vs. building our own. 
  • Working to create user stories around Notify People and the various security concerns identified by Monterrey Peninsula College.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • For user stories related to notifying people and permissions/roles, I'll reach out to Bryce and Joan for assistance. Bryce designed the original Notify People feature and has valuable insights into how it was developed, as well as how we might address concerns about visibility of personas, space, and profile fields by non-admin users who use Notify People.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • I learned a lot about our ghostwriting feature and some of the challenges our customers face when using it. I believe we have several opportunities to improve this feature, increasing transparency and enhancing the overall user experience to make it more intuitive.
  • I had several huddles this past week and found them very helpful for quickly tackling questions and taking action on items. Sometimes, it's easier to have a conversation than try to convey an idea or answer a question via Slack message.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: July 25th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Worked with Wendi to connect with Bartlett and CHG about Journeys to provide some additional getting-started information and hear any questions or concerns they currently have
  • Worked with CSMs to get remaining customers interested in beta testing scheduled to receive site updates for Journeys
  • Worked with Wendi and Dave to address a bug in Journeys that is preventing the Custom Content Type Step from being completed in any order. Currently, adding this step type blocks progress as the step type is set to "required" and can not be changed.
  • Worked with Anh Tran to resolve discrepancies between the PRD and various user stories
  • Collaborated with Anh Tran on our approach to developing the prototype, utilizing merge requests in GitLab and preview branch deploys on Netlify to better align with user stories in DevOps. This approach enables us to track and preview incremental changes while ensuring that our main branch and prototype accurately reflect the current scope of the feature, thereby preventing confusion for the development team.
  • Worked on user stories to allow journey participants to view content from private spaces without needing to be a member of the space, and to automate participant enrollment
  • wrote user stories and acceptance criteria for a new Persona filter added to the User Activity report, and the addition of Bookmarks as part of our content Analytics.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Nothing at the moment

Priorities for the next week:

  • Scheduled follow-up calls with customers to gather feedback and uncover usability issues with Journeys
  • Call scheduled with UALR to get them started with Journeys
  • Continued work and collaboration with the team to understand James Hardies challenges around search analytics and to develop a solution for their challenges

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Continued collaboration with CSMs on Journeys
  • Continued collaboration with Dev on Newsletters

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Always learning new ways to improve our processes as we work to develop new features.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: July 18th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Worked with Jennifer and Luis on a new process for sizing user stories for large features like Newsletters. We broke down each requirement into its own User Story in DevOps so we could get a better idea of which requirements might take more time and effort than others. This will help us to adjust the scope to more effectively balance value and effort.
  • Finalized the reduced requirements for Newsletters MVP. Rewrote and reorganized the PRD and user stories
  • Reviewed Anh Tran's latest merge requests. Provided feedback on his questions regarding the differences between the technical specs and the PRD
  •  Worked on scoping new features/enhancements for Journeys
    • Granular content permissions that allow individual content-level access to users in a journey without granting them access to private spaces.
    • Automated participant enrollment
  • Joined a call with Wendi to set up Journeys for Peerage

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No major blockers at this time.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continue working on new feature/enhancements for journeys
  • Work with Anh Tran on a new process to use the preview server on Netlify and merge request to manage new features and enhancements added to Journeys
  • Continued work on Newsletters, adding additional details and acceptance criteria to user stories for MVP.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Will continue working with CSMs to set up customer feedback calls for Journeys

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • I believe we're making progress in refining our processes as we learn from each new release what works well and what needs improvement.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: July 11th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Revised the scope of the Newsletters MVP, removing templates, settings, and analytics for now. Seeking guidance from Development to understand which requirements are expanding the user story so I can better focus the scope while still including features our users will find valuable.
  • Did some research and used Windsurf to help suggest some basic analytics we should include in our MVP based on our use of Mailgun and current email configuration documentation. I provided this outline, which includes implementation steps with Mailgun, SMTP, and Outlook configurations for review to see if these analytics are feasible for MVP and if we can implement them as Windsurf suggests.
  • Created user stories to enable Unicode Emoji Characters in our web app, reducing reliance on image assets. This aligns with modern web standards and ensures consistency across web and mobile platforms, as emojis will now be rendered natively by devices in both environments.
  • Created multiple bugs and user stories to address UI/UX issues in Journeys for the upcoming sprint, focusing on usability problems and inconsistencies discovered after using Journeys for a while.
  • Worked with CSMs to get customer's updated requests in so they can start testing Journeys beta and provide feedback

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continued focus on Newsletters. Reviewing prototype revisions and working with dev to align the tech design and scope for MVP so we can launch this feature by the end of Q3
  • Continued creation of bugs/user stories for Journeys 
  • Scoping additional user stories to be included in the upcoming sprint
  • Continued collaboration with CSMs to update customer sites to include Journeys beta

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • I will need support from the dev team to understand the requirements impacting the size of this user story User Story 35593: Tech Design - Email Newsletters, so I can more accurately adjust the scope of the MVP to ensure a launch by the end of Q3
  • Will need to continue working closely with CSMs to update customers' sites for Journey beta testing

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • We need to include development early on in the process when creating the product requirements for a feature. This will enable a more agile approach. Currently, we are being delayed due to a waterfall process where developers wait for product to finalize the scope and requirements before providing a technical design estimate. This means we won't know what needs to be cut or should be cut until development spends a few weeks doing technical design. Since the scope also depends on input from developers about what is feasible within the timeframe, it's essential for developers to be involved in the scoping process at the start of these large projects. This helps prevent ending up in this back-and-forth waterfall process that creates a bottleneck.

 

 

", "ContentBodyUnprocessed": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: June 20, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • The analytics page for the newsletter prototype is now complete, and Anh Tran is implementing the necessary permissions.
  • I gave four customer demos of the prototype, which received strong positive feedback. Customers found the UI/UX intuitive and better than the current tools. They highlighted the need for separate distribution lists and easier ways to add intranet content to newsletters. The MVP will use @mentions, but deeper content integration is a future opportunity.
  • Journeys QA is on track to finish this week. Most bugs found are low priority, and I am reviewing and prioritizing new issues.
  • Documentation is moving forward with Vladana.
  • Go-to-market planning is progressing. Megan and I met on Monday to discuss next steps.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No major blockers at this time.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Work with Anh on the newsletter feature:
    • Revise the UI/UX and add more details to user stories.
    • Address newsletter templates, audiences/distribution lists, and additional settings.
  • Review outstanding questions and suggestions from Journeys QA, which will be completed by June 20.
  • Make sure Anh and the team have what they need to keep making progress on Newsletters while I am out on PTO.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • No additional support needed at this time. Megan will reach out if she needs help or resources.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Customer demos showed that separating distribution lists from personas and making intranet content easier to add are important needs. These will guide future improvements.
  • I will be out June 30–July 4 for a move and will return July 7.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: June 13, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Reviewed all new bugs related to Journeys, provided feedback, and tagged them by severity. Suggestions are still pending review.
  • Reviewed email newsletter champion feedback.
  • Helped Anh Tran with the Supabase issue for email newsletters and discussed next steps.
  • Anh now has repo access and made his first changes, addressing Adam’s suggestions.
  • Had a positive customer call with Mariner Finance about migrating their email creation and management to Axero. They liked the UI/UX and drag-and-drop builder.
  • Connected with Megan about go-to-market strategy and shared research and suggestions. Set up a call for next week.
  • Connected with Vladana as she started on documentation.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Worked to resolve the Supabase issue and find a better approach for database usage with prototypes
  • There are many bugs and questions regarding both Journeys and Email Newsletters. These are large, complex projects that are high priority and require urgency to complete quickly. Managing these two large projects feels challenging. 

Priorities for the next week:

  • Finish reviewing champion feedback for the newsletter.
  • Review more bugs and suggestions for Journeys.
  • Work with Anh on the email newsletter prototype to complete user stories and address feedback.
  • Hold customer calls to demo the email newsletter prototype for PGA Superstore, United Way of Greater Cincinnati, and the University of Wisconsin–Madison.
  • Have a GTM call with Megan
  • Continue supporting the QA/Dev team with bugs for Journeys.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Need to clarify which developers will be assigned to the newsletter project to schedule a team kickoff and ensure good communication.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Managing two urgent projects at once is challenging and requires clear priorities.
  • Early and clear communication with stakeholders helps to ensure alignment on the products and features we design.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: June 6, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

    • This week, I worked on the email newsletters prototype. I added drag and drop features, a header, and a footer to the email canvas.
    • I made updates to the text blocks so it is now possible to edit rich text right in the email canvas.
    • I worked to improve the email dashboard, adding a table view, filtering and sorting options, and the ability to duplicate emails.
    • I improved the look and feel of the navigation sidebar in the email editor, adding tabs. for email settings, design, and recipients.
    • I also spent time updating and adding user stories, revising the product requirements document, and adding additional details on how to use the document and where to find information, in preparation for handing this off to development
    • I had a very productive call with the Dev and QA teams to review the Journeys feature. We learned a lot during the call and I plan to have these types of calls earlier in the development process

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • No major challenges or roadblocks to report this week.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Next week, I will wrap up my work on the email newsletter prototype. I still need to add the Analytics section, User Permissions section, and a Settings section. I should have this ready by Monday.
  • I then plan to shift my focus to assisting with Journeys now that QA is testing it. There have been many questions that have already come up that will need my attention

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • I will be needing help from the team to set up calls with customers to look at the email newsletter prototype.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • I had a call with the Dev and QA team to review the work that has been done on Journeys. This call was very helpful as it raised good questions and the team provided a lot of insight and feedback. We will start having calls like this earlier in the process so questions and feedback can be addressed before and during development rather than after.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: May 30th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Worked on the Email Newsletter PRD
    • Added additional detailed user stories for content blocks (text, images, headings, buttons) 
    • Added user stories for arranging, editing, and creating content blocks
    • Added user stories for auto-saving email drafts
    • Added user stories for cloning emails
    • Added additional user stories for sending, scheduled sending, test sending, and managing sending to external email domains (Gmail, Yahoo, etc.)
    • Added additional detailed user stories for email analytics.
      • Summaries for sent, delivered, open, and click-through rates.
      • Detailed analytics for specific emails, total recipients, sent, delivered, undelivered (bounced, opted out, blocked), opened, CTR, and unique clicks.
      • Dedicated analytics page, including visual representation of key metrics
      • Status log for individual recipients (email address, status [sent, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, opted out, blocked] time stamp)
      • Exporting Analytics
    • Added detailed user stories for permissions
      • Dedicated permissions management for Email Newsletters
      • Unique roles (Email admin, email manager, email author, email viewer)
      • Audit logs for permission and role changes
      • Create and manage custom email roles
      • Bulk assign roles
    • To ensure the various teams have early insight and input on the newsletter feature and to be able to plan their work, I shared the PRD with other team members to gather feedback and insight (Megan, Joan, Jennifer, Isha)
      • I've been addressing and incorporating this feedback to help refine this feature and ensure the user stories are detailed enough and address the desired features and functionality for the MVP
    • Created and worked on prototypes using windsurf and lovable.
      • incorporated a process to have the AI develop a task list based on the user stories and PRD. Then, it utilizes this task list to build the prototype and track progress as it progresses. This phased approach has yielded promising results

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No real roadblocks. The challenge is adding enough functionality to the MVP to make it worthwhile for our customers to use, engage with, and provide feedback on without making the scope so big that it takes more effort than the value it provides, or we spend time developing functionality our customers don't want or need. This is why I have invited other team members to share their ideas in the early stages of developing this feature, so we can address edge cases and identify user needs that I may not have initially thought of. There is strength in numbers.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continue developing and refining user stories for Newsletters
  • Continue developing and refining the email newsletter prototype.
  • Support Dev and QA on any items that come up regarding Journeys.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • I'll continue working with Jennifer, Megan, Isha, and Joan to make sure their questions regarding email newsletters are addressed and their feedback is considered, and they have the information they need to support this feature as we get closer to developing it.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • I found a useful video that provided some useful prompts that helped me with the latest email newsletter prototype. This allowed me to leverage the AI to help define a plan for developing and implementing the prototype and then progressing in phases in a step-by-step manner, ensuring that there were checkpoints along the way to address any issues. So far, the results have been a big improvement.

The challenge is adding enough functionality to the MVP to make it worthwhile for our customers to use, engage with, and provide feedback on without making the scope so big that it takes more effort than the value it provides, or we spend time developing functionality our customers don't want or need. This is why I have invited other team members to share their ideas in the early stages of developing this feature, so we can address edge cases and identify user needs that I may not have initially thought of. There is strength in numbers.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continue developing and refining user stories for Newsletters
  • Continue developing and refining the email newsletter prototype.
  • Support Dev and QA on any items that come up regarding Journeys.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • I'll continue working with Jennifer, Megan, Isha, and Joan to make sure their questions regarding email newsletters are addressed and their feedback is considered, and they have the information they need to support this feature as we get closer to developing it.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • I found a useful video that provided some useful prompts that helped me with the latest email newsletter prototype. This allowed me to leverage the AI to help define a plan for developing and implementing the prototype and then progressing in phases in a step-by-step manner, ensuring that there were checkpoints along the way to address any issues. So far, the results have been a big improvement.

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: May 23, 2025

  • Proposed and documented three MVP options for the Email Newsletter, each with requirements and user stories.

  • Started work in Windsurf on the new newsletter prototype, building the foundation for the dashboard, permissions, and email editor.
  • Drafted an outline for expanding the drag-and-drop interface to other parts of the platform, now in a new PRD tab.
  • Completed daily reviews of Pod 1.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Some of the technical considerations for Email Newsletters will require input from others. I shared the document with Joan to get feedback.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continue working on the email newsletter PRD and user stories as I get more feedback.
  • Help address any Journey-related questions 

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Will require input from Joan on email newsletters.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • No specific lessons learned or opportunities for improvement

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: Friday, May 16, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Wrote user stories for the first version of Newsletters and made a draft of the product requirements document.
  • Made a competitor analysis for Newsletters.
  • Answered questions from Minh and Anh Le about Journeys.
  • Worked on the Newsletter prototype in Windsurf.
  • Replied to customers who want to join the Journeys beta test.
  • Reviewed and updated user story 33442 to help the development team size the work.
  • Responded to comments on user story 34361.
  • Worked on a supporting document for Newsletters, focusing on the main features for the MVP and revising user stories.
  • Gathered recordings, transcripts, and notes from customer talks about email and newsletters to review next week.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No major challenges or roadblocks this week.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Keep working on the Newsletter supporting document and update it with new user stories for the MVP.
  • Review user interviews, notes, transcripts for email newsletters
  • Update the Journeys UI and clear up confusion with the prototype.
  • Continue daily product work, reviewing bugs, user stories, and scoping.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • No support needed at this time.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • No new lessons learned this week.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: May 9th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Contacted customers about the Journeys Beta program, confirmed participation from 50% of those contacted.
  • Supported the completion of Journeys, worked on addressing UI details for Minh (UI is near completion), and coordinated with Anh Le (backend is almost done).
  • Worked on the design system by prototyping a site, cloning the codebase, and updating Figma with color tokens.
  • Created and prepared Journeys slides for Marci and Sales, with Megan’s assistance.
  • Participated in regular meetings: Axero Monday standup, QA-Dev team meeting, Experience team weekly meeting, feature grooming and sprint planning, and 1:1 with Jennifer.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Managing the Journey Beta program, worked with Jennifer to outline the process for rolling out the beta to customers and scheduling feedback sessions 
  • Determining ROI for user stories where the ROI may not be obvious, Jennifer provided guidance on how to quickly identify this

Priorities for the next week:

  • Finalize UI details for Minh to ensure the completion of Journeys.
  • Analyze Interact webinar questions and transcript to gain insights for the Newsletter Feature.
  • Focus on building user stories for Newsletters, particularly the wysiwyg editing experience, to address customer challenges with existing content tools.
  • Define the MVP scope for Newsletters.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • As we get closer to releasing the beta version of Journeys, I will be working closely with our experience team to schedule updates, training, and feedback sessions with customers.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Nothing to report this week
