5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: May, 2 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Created a form to collect a list of customers who want to be included in the Journeys Beta release.
  • Attended the weekly product grooming session and discussed plans for adding a menu item to access the Journey Manager.
  • Attended an email newsletter masterclass webinar hosted by Interact.
  • Supported Dev in completing the Journeys feature, focusing on the frontend. I updated Figma design files and user stories, created color tokens and icon components for Minh to use in the UI, and worked closely with them to refine designs and answer any questions.
  • Continued working on the design system project, including creating color tokens and starting to use AI to build a site for documenting and showing how to use the design system.
  • Scoped user stories, focusing on a story related to enterprise search, as this is a growing area in the competitive market.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No challenges or roadblocks to report.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continue working on Journeys and support Minh to complete the frontend UI.
  • Reach out to customers interested in Journeys so they can make plans with their CSM to update their site when the release is ready.
  • Work on the newsletters feature by reviewing existing user stories, notes, and prototypes, then revise and refine.
  • Continue working on the Design System.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • I will likely need to coordinate with Jennifer and our CSMs on reaching out to customers interested in Journeys

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • No new lessons learned or opportunities for improvement this week.

No specific lessons or opportunities for improvement to report this week.

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-april-18-2025", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers
Week ending: April 18, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Finalized the Employee Journeys presentation for the Dev and QA meeting and delivered it successfully.
  • Updated and improved the Journeys presentation for the business team meeting to help them share the feature with customers.
  • Continued daily product work, including reviewing bugs, checking user stories, and making sure developers could keep working.
  • Started working on the design system project, exploring new ideas now that I have access to the codebase.
  • Began planning for the next big feature, Email Newsletters, using what I learned from the Journeys project.
  • Worked closely with Anh Le on the Journeys feature.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No major roadblocks this week.

Priorities for the next week:

  • present the Journeys feature to the Business Team and support Sales and Marketing as they incorporate the information into their goto market strategy.
  • Scoping new user stories to build out our ongoing product roadmap, focusing on stories that have a big roi.
  • Keep working on the design system project.
  • Continue planning and organizing the Email Newsletters feature, using lessons from Journeys feature.
  • Continue daily product tasks, like reviewing bugs and helping the team.
  • Keep working with Anh Le and the team to make Journeys successful.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • No extra support is needed right now.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Using lessons from the Journeys project is helping me plan better for new features.
  • Working closely with team members like Anh Le makes the process smoother and helps solve problems faster.
  • Focusing on user stories with a high return on investment will help us build a stronger product roadmap.

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-april-11-2025", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: April 11, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Updated the Employee Journeys Presentation in preparation for the Dev and QA call happening next week.
  • Revised the Journeys design to align with the current functionality being implemented by the development team and the updated user stories. This process involved close collaboration with the development team and required an agile approach.
  • Gained valuable insights into the benefits of early collaboration with developers, which I plan to apply to future features like the Email Newsletter feature and Site Branding.
  • Identified how this collaborative process can support the development of a design system by incorporating input from all stakeholders who will use it in their daily work.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Keeping the Journeys design updates aligned with the development team’s implementation required consistent communication and coordination.
  • Ensuring the design revisions met both user needs and technical constraints was a collaborative effort that required ongoing adjustments.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Finalize the Employee Journeys Presentation for the Dev and QA meeting.
  • Update and enhance the presentation for the Sales and Marketing meeting to ensure they have the necessary information to position Journeys effectively in the market.
  • Continue daily product duties, including reviewing bugs, monitoring user stories, and ensuring developers are unblocked.
  • Begin work on the design system project, exploring different approaches now that I have access to the codebase.
  • Start planning for the next major feature, Email Newsletters, applying lessons learned from the Journeys feature to improve the process.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Input from the Sales and Marketing team to ensure the Journeys presentation addresses their needs for market positioning.
  • Continued collaboration with the development team to align on the design system project and upcoming features.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Early collaboration with developers is critical for aligning design and functionality, and I plan to incorporate this approach into future projects.
  • The iterative and collaborative process used for the Journeys feature has highlighted the importance of flexibility and open communication in achieving successful outcomes.
  • Engaging stakeholders early in the design system project will ensure it meets the needs of all users.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers
Week ending: Friday, March 28, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Completed daily Pod bug and user story reviews, ensuring smooth operations and progress.
  • Provided answers to developer questions about Journeys, emphasizing the importance of sharing user stories early to align with Agile practices.
  • Attended key meetings, including the QA-Dev team meeting, Experience team weekly meeting, Limited Release Demo for sprints 55/56/57, 1:1, Sprint Grooming and a Marketing meeting.
  • Delivered details for the custom content type step and made updates to the Admin Prototype and Journeys End User experience.
  • Reviewed and clarified Anh Le’s questions about Journeys, providing additional details to improve understanding.
  • Made progress in the Coursera "Introduction to Web Development" course, completing Module 3.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • No specific challenges or roadblocks were noted this week.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Continue daily Pod reviews and attend the product team meeting.
  • Update Journeys Figma design files to reflect changes and details discussed in supporting documentation.
  • Continue progress in the Coursera web development course.
  • Work on presentation for Journys

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • No additional support is required at this time.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Sharing user stories with developers early fosters better collaboration and aligns with Agile practices.
  • Regularly reviewing and clarifying questions from team members ensures smoother communication and progress.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers
Week ending: March 21, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Met with STAC to learn about their onboarding process and shared the Journeys prototype, gaining insights into how the feature can support external users, not just employees.

  • Updated the Journeys supporting documentation and answered questions from Anh Le to clarify the Admin experience.

  • Continued refining the Journeys Admin prototype by removing non-MVP elements.

  • Worked on key components for the End User experience, including the Journey dashboard, notifications, reminders, and pop-ups.

  • Attended a demo with Tri-City Medical Center.

  • Made progress in the Coursera Introduction to Web Development course.

  • Completed daily Web Pod 1 reviews of bugs, completed user stories, and new fixes.

  • Attend the Builder Labs webinar on optimizing Figma for AI-powered prototyping.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

No major roadblocks this week.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Finalize the Journeys Admin prototype updates for MVP 1.

  • Continue refining the End User UI, adding necessary interactions like step completion and dashboard views.

  • Continue progress on the Coursera web development course.

  • Attend wiz.io demo

Collaboration and Support Needed:

No additional support is needed at this time.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Gaining insights from customers like STAC is helping shape Journeys into a more flexible feature that serves a variety of use cases.

  • Refining how we document and clarify features ensures smoother collaboration with stakeholders and developers.

  • Met with STAC to learn about their onboarding process and shared the Journeys prototype, gaining insights into how the feature can support external users, not just employees.

  • Updated the Journeys supporting documentation and answered questions from Anh Le to clarify the Admin experience.

  • Continued refining the Journeys Admin prototype by removing non-MVP elements.

  • Worked on key components for the End User experience, including the Journey dashboard, notifications, reminders, and pop-ups.

  • Attended a demo with Tri-City Medical Center.

  • Made progress in the Coursera Introduction to Web Development course.

  • Completed daily Web Pod 1 reviews of bugs, completed user stories, and new fixes.

  • Attend the Builder Labs webinar on optimizing Figma for AI-powered prototyping.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

No major roadblocks this week.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Finalize the Journeys Admin prototype updates for MVP 1.

  • Continue refining the End User UI, adding necessary interactions like step completion and dashboard views.

  • Continue progress on the Coursera web development course.

  • Attend wiz.io demo

Collaboration and Support Needed:

No additional support is needed at this time.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Gaining insights from customers like STAC is helping shape Journeys into a more flexible feature that serves a variety of use cases.

  • Refining how we document and clarify features ensures smoother collaboration with stakeholders and developers.

  • Observing discovery calls continues to be valuable for understanding customer challenges and potential feature improvements.

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-march-7-2025", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers
Week ending: March 7, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

  • Made significant progress on the Journey Admin UI, including refining the Add Step modal for better readability and usability.
  • Developed a custom step type for rich text input, requiring layout adjustments.
  • Improved Journey User Management, creating a dedicated page, adding search and filtering options, and refining the user enrollment experience.
  • Ensured all Journey building blocks are functional, including improvements to the notification step.
  • Completed daily Web Pod reviews of bugs and attended Feature Grooming & Sprint Planning.
  • Spent time in Coursera’s JavaScript course to continue strengthening my technical skills.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

  • Scaling back some features for MVP 1 to simplify the initial release and allow more time to address complex challenges.

Priorities for the next week:

  • Finalize Journey Admin UI/UX for MVP 1.
  • Refine and simplify the End User UI/UX for MVP 1.
  • Work on Journey specifications documentation and supporting materials.
  • Continue progress on the Coursera JavaScript course.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

  • Will work with Jennifer on supporting documentation for Journeys.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

  • Discovered that Windsurf can store past conversations, allowing for better continuity when restarting chats.
  • Continuing to prioritize scaling features appropriately for MVP 1 to balance speed and complexity.

 

I recently got back into playing Magic: The Gathering after being invited by some friends to play. I haven't played this game since 1995, and it's different from how it used to be, but it's still fun!

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: February 21, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • I updated the prototype and designs for the Journeys feature based on customer research and feedback to prepare for frontend development.

  • I reviewed and gathered key documents (old PRD, competitive analysis, admin functionality specs, user stories, and technical documentation) to identify the MVP requirements for the feature.

  • I identified missing requirements, UI/UX changes based on customer feedback, and enhancements based on competitor analysis to ensure the feature is competitive and user-friendly.

  • I refined the Journey Builder interface, improving clarity and usability.

  • I updated terminology and naming conventions to make the feature more intuitive for users.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Balancing design updates with usability improvements required careful testing to maintain a smooth workflow.

  • Ensuring that all MVP requirements were properly documented and accounted for took extra effort but was necessary to prevent gaps in development.

  • The design mockups and prototypes still need final refinements before handoff to development.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Finalize all design assets, prototypes, and documentation to prepare for handoff to the development team.

  • Review user story 30777 related to the mobile chat feature to assist Vanessa as needed.

  • Ensure the Journeys UI and UX updates are fully aligned with the identified MVP requirements.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • I connected with {{mention:2053:15}} , regarding mobile app styling. Since she is focused on the chat feature, this is temporarily on hold, but I reached out to offer assistance on other tasks.

  • At her request, I will review user story 30777 this week to see how I can contribute to the mobile chat feature.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Refining MVP requirements early ensures that all necessary functionality is captured before development begins.

  • Balancing design updates with usability improvements takes extra time but results in a better user experience.

 

We had a bunch of snow this past week

Because of this we mainly stayed in doors so Howdy has had a bad case of the Zoomies so I took him to PetsMart and bought him a bunch of toys and treats.

5-15 Report

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Continued refining the journeys prototype, improving functionality, and ensuring the UI aligns with expected user behavior.
  • Reviewed and provided feedback on completed development user stories, bugs, and new issues reported by QA.
  • Finalized UI/UX assets for the white-label mobile app customization feature
  • Completed module 1 and started module 2 in the Coursera Introduction to Web Development course.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Encountered an issue with Windsurf AI overwriting previous work while making changes to the prototype, requiring me to revert to a previous commit. Learned the importance of making small, incremental changes and committing frequently to avoid setbacks.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Finalize the journey prototype, incorporating user feedback.
  • Work with Victoria on user research for the journeys prototype with Leading Educators.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • No real support is needed but I will be working with Victoria to conduct user research for the journeys prototype with Leading Educators.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Continue making progress on Coursera Introduction to Web Development

  • Continue working on Journey's Admin prototype.
  • Finish up UI/UX the white-label mobile app customization feature
  • Coursera training

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Nothing at the moment

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Made positive progress with Windsurf AI and prompt engineering by researching best practices and learning from other product managers.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

Priorities for the next week:

  • Strengthening collaboration with QA, Dev, and the mobile product team has provided deeper insight into implementation challenges, allowing for more effective and efficient product development.

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-january-24th-2025", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: January 24th, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Collaborated closely with Jennifer to understand and implement the new daily operations for product management.
  • Set up the Newsletter feature in both GitLab and Netlify to prepare for further development.
  • Spent time learning to use Windsurf and made incremental updates to the Newsletter feature, focusing on improvements to usability and functionality.
  • Refined the usability testing scenarios for Journeys to ensure they align with our research goals and account for new feedback.
  • Conducted user research calls with Press Ganey and Upstate Vets, gathering insights to inform ongoing product improvements.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Navigating Windsurf has presented some challenges, but I've discovered a variety of online tutorials that have helped me learn how to craft effective AI prompts. Although there's been a fair amount of trial and error involved, this process has provided valuable insights and learning opportunities. I'm actively seeking ways to streamline my approach, which I believe will lead to even better results in the future.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Continue refining and enhancing the Newsletter prototype using Windsurf.
  • Write a detailed User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and create UI/UX assets for the feature allowing customers to customize White-Label Mobile App colors via the Web App’s Control Panel.
  • Conduct a user research call with HECU to gather additional feedback and insights for ongoing projects.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Any additional resources or tips for improving efficiency when working with Windsurf would be helpful. If others have experience with similar tools, I’d appreciate hearing about their approaches.
  • Continued alignment with Jennifer and other stakeholders to ensure we’re meeting product goals and capturing meaningful customer insights.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • I’ve realized the importance of dedicating time upfront to understand new tools like Windsurf better. While trial and error can lead to progress, more structured learning could save time and effort in the long run.
  • The user research calls this week emphasized the value of tailoring our usability scenarios to capture specific pain points and opportunities. Continuing to refine these will ensure more actionable insights moving forward.

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-january-17-2025", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: January, 17th 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Developed and refined usability testing scenarios for the Journeys feature.
  • Conducted a usability test with Rebecca from PGA Super Store, which provided valuable insights into the user experience for the Admin portion of the Journey's feature.
  • Participated in a Stitch Fix call, observing Jennifer’s demo of the Journeys admin feature and taking notes on customer feedback.
  • Debriefed with Jennifer after the usability testing session to analyze findings and identify next steps.
  • Met with Brandon to understand custom work and workarounds he implements for clients, uncovering potential opportunities for new features or improvements.
  • Collaborated with Wendi to identify potential clients for Journeys research and explored how her work identifying at-risk customers could guide product improvements.
  • Learned about STAC’s onboarding process during a meeting with Eric to gather insights into how they use the Axero platform to onboard new users
  • Made revisitons to the Journeys frontend UI to address the latest feedback and requirements.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Continue refining usability testing scenarios based on insights from the PGA and Stich Fix session.
  • Make further updates to the Journeys UI based on usability testing feedback.
  • Focus on scheduling additional usability tests with other clients to learn and gather new insights for the Journeys Admin feature.
  • Meet with Press Ganey to conduct user research for the journeys admin ffeature
  • Work with Jennifer on new operational procedures and on Windsurf
  • Dedicate time to advancing work on the newsletter feature
  • Begin analyzing findings from usability tests and client interviews to present actionable recommendations.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Coordination with the CS team to identify and connect with more clients who would be strong candidates for usability tests or customer interviews.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • The usability test with Rebecca highlighted areas where the Journeys UI could be more intuitive, such as task organization and step descriptions. This reaffirmed the importance of clear labeling and meaningful user feedback in the interface.
  • Watching Jennifer’s conversation with Stitch Fix showed how client interactions can uncover both gaps and opportunities, highlighting the importance of structured follow-up questions to gain deeper insights from feedback.
  • Working with Wendi on indicators for at-risk customers offered a fresh perspective on aligning user challenges with feature development priorities, suggesting a more proactive approach to identifying pain points.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • The usability test with Rebecca highlighted areas where the Journeys UI could be more intuitive, such as task organization and step descriptions. This reaffirmed the importance of clear labeling and meaningful user feedback in the interface.
  • Watching Jennifer’s conversation with Stitch Fix showed how client interactions can uncover both gaps and opportunities, highlighting the importance of structured follow-up questions to gain deeper insights from feedback.
  • Working with Wendi on indicators for at-risk customers offered a fresh perspective on aligning user challenges with feature development priorities, suggesting a more proactive approach to identifying pain points.

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-january-3-2025", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: January 3, 2025

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • User Research:

    • Followed up with Callen-Lorde, HartBeat, Denver Center for the Performing Arts, and United Way of Greater Cincinnati (UWGC) to schedule user interviews for the email newsletter feature.
    • Scheduled and conducted a user research interview with Kristen Ealy from UWGC, gathering valuable insights into their needs for email newsletters.

  • Journeys Frontend UI Updates:

    • Began implementing updates to accommodate new admin requirements, including:
      • Ordered steps/step groups.
      • Steps outside of step groups.
      • Manual step completion.
      • Notifications and reminders.
      • Handling user experience impacts when journeys are edited or deleted (e.g., adding/removing/reordering steps).

  • Design System Work:

    • Audited the existing stylesheet to identify CSS custom properties currently in use.
    • Analyzed the frequency of custom property usage to focus on the most prominent styles.
    • Consolidated redundant and similar colors into a narrower, consistent palette.
    • Created an initial draft of a design system, starting with foundational tokens for colors, typography, and spacing.
    • Developed a JSON-based design token system and generated a new set of CSS variables with implementation documentation.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Several customers were out of the office for the holidays, delaying the ability to schedule additional calls. 

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Finalize updates to the Journeys frontend UI to meet new admin requirements.
  • Continue refining the design system, expanding on the draft stylesheet and integrating feedback.
  • Follow up with remaining customers to schedule user interviews for the email newsletter research.
  • Begin summarizing findings from completed user interviews to prepare for the next phase of the research process.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Feedback on the initial draft of the design system and suggestions for additional areas of improvement or expansion. Testing with the new Windsurf AI to see if it can style an app using the design system

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Auditing the stylesheet revealed opportunities to consolidate and simplify our design tokens, which will streamline future updates and ensure consistency. Creating a scalable system early on will save time as we expand the design system.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: December 27, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Learned and used Windsurf, an AI coding tool, to create an email newsletter prototype. 
  • Focused most of the week on the prototype, leaving little time for analyzing user research or updating the Journeys UI. With Christmas Eve and Christmas Day off, it was a short work week.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Windsurf had a steeper learning curve than expected, which slowed progress on other priorities. Just a lot of trial and error
  • No user research calls or analysis were completed due to the holidays and my focus on the prototype.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Update the Journeys UI to accommodate new requirements for steps outside of step groups.
  • Consolidate and report on findings from email newsletter research as more calls are scheduled.
  • Draft a design system to align components and improve consistency.
  • Update the user research space with the most recent findings.
  • Provide newsletter support if requested by Jennifer.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Connected with Customer Success Managers to encourage them to mention the email newsletter research to clients they recommended during their conversations.
    • Received a follow-up response from Kristen at United Way of Greater Cincinnati, so I’ll work on scheduling a call with her.
    • Alvina from Hartbeat is out of the office until January 6th; I’ll reconnect after she returns.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Learning a new tool like Windsurf is rewarding but requires dedicated time, especially during a short workweek.
  • Planning for holiday schedules is critical to maintain momentum on projects and deadlines.

 

The first REAL tree I've had in many years...it barely fit!

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Learning a new tool like Windsurf is rewarding but requires dedicated time, especially during a short workweek.
  • Planning for holiday schedules is critical to maintain momentum on projects and deadlines.

 

The first REAL tree I've had in many years...it barely fit!

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week Ending: December 13, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Research:

    • Revised the interview guide based on feedback and learnings from earlier sessions.
    • Conducted three customer interviews (PGA Super Store, Hartbeat, and Mariner Finance).
    • Collaborated with {{mention:3575:15}} , {{mention:2392:15}} , and {{mention:3764:15}}  for note-taking and post-interview insights.
    • Began reviewing and summarizing findings to identify key insights for the email newsletter feature.

  • Design:

    • Presented updated Journeys UI at a second design critique and gathered additional feedback for refinement.
    • Identified new questions about edge cases, such as handling journeys with no active steps and adding steps to completed journeys.

  • Collaboration:

    • Attended a demo with Lemoine to better understand customer needs when evaluating intranet platforms.
    • Initiated plans to make attending sales calls a regular part of my workflow

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Forgot to start recording during the Mariner Finance interview
    • Resolution: Configured Zoom to automatically record all meetings and ensured a process to obtain consent before the call.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Complete revisions to the Journeys UI, addressing questions from the design critique.
  • Continue user research on the email newsletter feature, including scheduling and conducting additional interviews.
  • Summarize findings from completed interviews and identify emerging themes and trends.
  • Plan the next design critique session and refine the Journeys UI based on feedback.
  • Begin integrating insights from customer interviews into the product requirements document for the newsletter feature.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Note-Taking Collaboration: Amy, Alex, and Megan have been invaluable as note-takers during interviews, contributing additional insights. Continued support from others in this area will be essential for upcoming interviews.
  • Customer Success Coordination: The CS team's support in identifying more customers for interviews is critical as I aim to expand the participant pool for this research. I will be updating the submission form to make this process even easier.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Process Improvement: The oversight in recording the Mariner Finance call showed the need for better preparation. Despite setting up multiple reminders to record calls, the interesting conversation distracted me from this step. Implementing automatic Zoom recordings is a direct improvement to avoid similar issues in the future. I'll make sure we get consent before calls to maintain ethical standards for user research.
  • Expanding Perspectives: Attending the sales demo with Lemoine helped me better understand customer needs, highlighting the value of cross-functional learning. I plan to make this a regular part of my workflow which will improve my understanding of user expectations.
  • Clarifying Design Requirements: Feedback from the design critique showed the importance of defining edge cases early in the process. Questions such as how to handle inactive journeys or adding steps to completed ones will guide future iterations.

 

I've been feeling very nostalgic this holiday season so I treated myself to some items from my favorite Christmas memories. Growing up, I remember these being one of my favorite decorations my grandma had and I loved playing with them.  I found these on eBay practically brand new in the original box! I definitely paid more than the $18 sticker price though!

I attached a video showing how annoying these really are, but I still love them!

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: December 6, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Research:

    • Revised the research guide and clarified goals and questions for the email newsletter project.
    • Scheduled user interviews with PGA Superstore and Mariner Finance, and contacted additional customers (Hartbeat, UWGC, Callen Lorde).
    • Wrote documentation on research methods and conducted contextual walkthrough workshops.
    • Created a list of potential customers to interview using database queries and feedback forums.
    • Developed plans for note-taking templates to involve the team in interviews.

  • Design Work:

    • Updated the Journeys UI with revised colors and created a prototype for critique participants.
    • Reviewed and provided feedback on the process for the upcoming Journey Admin UI design critiques, and some proposed updates to the people directory in the mobile app.

  • Team Contributions:

    • Participated in the sprint retrospective for insights into process improvement. This helped me understand how the broader development team is functioning.
    • Worked with CS and team members to streamline the process of identifying customers for research. Also worked with {{mention:3562:15}} {{mention:370:15}} and {{mention:4996:15}} to identify other avenues for finding prospective customers to contact

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • I received limited responses from the initial group of customers I contacted for user interviews, this seems to necessitate reaching out to a larger pool of candidates.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Complete revisions to the interview guide for the email newsletter research.
  • Conduct scheduled interviews with PGA Superstore and Mariner Finance.
  • Finalize the note-taking template and share it with the team for collaborative interview sessions.
  • Make minor updates to the Journeys frontend UI and prototype, focusing on ensuring Dev has the necessary context and specifications to implement the user experience and interface as designed.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • The CS team has been instrumental in identifying customers for research. The new form I created received positive feedback, making it easier for busy team members to contribute contacts. Aggregating this information into a structured repository can streamline future research efforts.

  • I’d like a co-worker to join the user interviews as a note-taker and observer. This will improve data collection and allow me to focus on facilitation while giving team members insight into the research process and its impact on our product and company direction.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Positive feedback from the CS team highlighted the usefulness of the new form for submitting customer contacts. This reinforced the importance of simplifying processes to increase participation. Suggestions to make it easier to add multiple contacts per company revealed an opportunity for further refinement.
  • Questions to Answer:
    • How can we improve response rates from customers for research participation?
    • Are there additional incentives or strategies to encourage customer engagement with our research process?

 

First time using our new SoloStove Mesa, all we had was fat wood so the flame was HUGE but it was fun and the S'mores were delicious 

 

We're also getting into the Christmas spirit. This is my dog Howdy! I adopted him a couple of months ago after fostering him for a few weeks. It was difficult to adopt again after my dog Cooper passed a couple of years ago, but Howdy has brought me a lot of love and joy.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week Ending: November 29, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Drafted a research plan for the email newsletter research project.
  • Reviewed user stories for the email newsletter feature to develop research questions and hypotheses.
  • Created a Google Form and spreadsheet to collect customer contact information for scheduling user interviews.
  • Drafted an interview guide and interview questions for the email newsletter research.
  • Provided feedback on the Mobile App Styling user story.
  • Worked with the Customer Success (CS) team to begin collecting customer contact information for the email newsletter research.
  • Sent out the first set of email invitations for customer interviews

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • A slow start to receiving customer contacts from the CS team due to the holiday week. This may delay the user interviews if support is not maintained.
  • To mitigate this, I started identifying potential customers myself by reviewing ideas, suggestions, and cases related to email, newsletters, and bulk communication. I’ll ask CS team members to review these contacts for confirmation before reaching out.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Revise the interview questions for the email newsletter research.
  • Update the research plan based on feedback or additional insights.
  • Continue gathering customer contact information, working closely with CS and independently identifying candidates.
  • Send additional email invitations to schedule user interviews for the research project.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Continued support from the CS team in providing customer contact information to prevent delays in scheduling user interviews.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • having multiple strategies for recruiting customers for user research will help this process going forward.

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: November 22, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Revised the Journeys frontend user interface:

    • Added elements to indicate when steps, segments, and journeys are completed.
    • Enhanced details to showcase user progress within a journey.
    • Provided additional context to make filters easier to use and understand based on feedback from a design critique.
    • Adjusted the appearance of journey cards to better distinguish them from segments and step cards.

  • Reviewed Brandon's white label app form, providing user experience and interface feedback.

  • Met with Malia to discuss ABM’s custom app and explore opportunities for customer expansion.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • No major blockers were this week (other than a cold🤒).

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Finalize updates to the Journeys frontend user interface based on remaining feedback.

  • Prepare for user research on the Email newsletter feature:

    • Develop research questions and interview scripts.
    • Coordinate with the CS team to schedule customer interviews for the following week or as soon as feasible.

  • Review and provide feedback on Vanessa’s mobile app customization user story.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Assistance from the CS team is needed to schedule customer interviews for email newsletter feature validation.

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Peer reviews for Brandon's form show how important teamwork is in improving designs.

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-november-15-2024", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel Stiers

Week ending: November 15, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Continued working on updates to the Journeys UI based on feedback from the design critique. Made refinements to meet user needs better.
  • worked on slides for the "Success Management" project, incorporating customer feedback, the Jobs-To-Be-Done theory, and Axero’s business goals. Updated the slides on personas and required reading management to include distinct problem statements and actionable solutions.
  • Reviewed customer feedback to identify key pain points, included quotes, and developed ideas for features such as persona-driven content targeting and streamlined required reading workflows.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Finding the right balance between knocking out immediate tasks and working on strategic projects.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Revise the journeys frontend UI and prepare design specifications for development, including any necessary annotations.
  • Conduct risk analysis for content exporting and perform a gap analysis to compare required reading with user journeys.
  • Examine how customers utilize spaces to navigate their intranet and collect additional data on how frontend users interact with these systems.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Nothing at the moment

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Using feedback loops during design critiques helps us find ways to improve early on. By involving different teams earlier in the design process, we can save time in the future.
  • Focusing on customer use-case research has proven valuable in uncovering actionable insights.

"nathaniel-week-ending-november-8", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel

Week ending: November 8th, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Continued progress on high-fidelity designs for the Journeys feature, integrating refined visuals and UI details.
  • Prepared for and led Axero's first design critique, including setting the agenda, defining scope, and crafting a customer persona for context. The critique with Alex, MeLissa, Dave, and Joan generated valuable, actionable feedback.
  • Developed a FigJam file summarizing the design’s objectives and user needs based on the Journeys PRD, which improved clarity and empathy among critique participants.
  • Drafted and iterated on the design critique structure, helping establish a new, repeatable process for receiving design feedback.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Nothing this week

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Revise the Journeys frontend UI by integrating insights and feedback from the design critique to better align with design goals and user needs.
  • Share findings from the design critique with the team, including a summary of actionable revisions.
  • Share the revised designs with the product team, internal stakeholders, and the development team for additional feedback.
  • Organize and prepare for customer feedback sessions to validate the revised Journeys UI.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • nothing at the moment

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • The design critique highlighted the importance of linking our discussions to specific design goals, which facilitates constructive feedback. It offered various perspectives on our design objectives for user journeys and revealed some usability issues that we can resolve before launching to customers. I believe that incorporating regular design critiques into our process will significantly enhance product quality and result in faster, more refined designs.

 

Here's a picture of some Cacio e pepe I made for dinner the other night, it came out ok with minimal clumping but I need to practice

"nathaniel-stiers-week-ending-november-1-2024", "ContentBody": "

5-15 Report

Name: Nathaniel

Week ending: November 1, 2024

Accomplishments for the week:

(List significant milestones and accomplishments from the week. This could include project completions, successful client interactions, implementation updates, or any positive developments)

  • Made progress on the high-fidelity wireframes for the Journeys feature, adding visual elements like copy and color.
  • Completed the Skills and Interest worksheet and reviewed the results to identify skill improvement opportunities, documenting actionable ideas for development.
  • Created and drafted internal documentation on key processes: design critique procedures, validating usability heuristics for UI design, and customer journey mapping. These documents will serve as resources for enhancing design quality and aligning UX best practices across the team.
  • Set up initial steps for a design critique session to gather feedback on the Journeys wireframes and initiated plans to schedule customer testing calls.

Challenges/Roadblocks:

(Describe obstacles, roadblocks, and or difficulties encountered during the week that may impede your intended tasks/goals.)

  • Awaiting feedback on the current wireframes to ensure they align with team expectations and customer goals.
  • Seeking clarity on the primary goals for additional user research on the Journeys feature. Specifically, if we’re focusing solely on design aesthetics or if we should evaluate the usability of flows and actions within the feature (i.e. creating a journey, adding steps, enrolling users, etc...), for this type of testing prototype development will be essential.

Priorities for the next week:

(Clearly outline the key tasks and objectives for the upcoming week. Be specific.)

  • Prepare high-fidelity designs for the Journeys feature for an internal design critique.
  • Organize and conduct a design critique session with cross-functional teams to gather feedback on Journeys and identify any improvements.
  • Continue refining the Hi-Fi wireframes based on internal feedback.
  • Prepare prototypes for usability testing, emphasizing customer actions and workflows like journey creation, user enrollment, and step completion.
  • Schedule customer calls to test design concepts and usability, aiming to gather insights that will help address key user needs and outcomes.

Collaboration and Support Needed:

(If there are areas where collaboration or support from other team members is required, explicitly mention them)

  • Input from cross-functional team members on high-fidelity wireframes to ensure alignment with user outcomes.
  • Guidance from product leadership on research goals for Journeys prototype testing with customers. What do we want to learn from our customers?

Lessons Learned/Opportunities for Improvement:

(Identify any areas that could be better; list questions you're working on answering; and share lessons you've recently learned or relearned.)

  • Engaging early with cross-functional team members for feedback is crucial to identifying whether designs effectively address user needs and outcomes.
  • Reading Lean UX by Jeff Gothelf & Josh Seiden, focusing on methods for Agile design processes and efficient cross-functional collaboration.

 

Photos from my recent trip to Connecticut with my girlfriend

Arrived in Boston and had dinner at Cheers

 

Stopped at the grave site of Ed and Lorraine Warren (paranormal investigators) on our road trip to Kent, CT. We ran into actual ghost hunters in the cemetery who helped us find their graves.

 

Found this REALY large donut in a contemporary art museum in Kent, CT. It's a french cruller which is my favorite donut. 

 

Went to a local cider mill and pumpkin patch. Picked out some pumpkins to carve.

 

And enjoyed the fall foliage

request.description:
### Get User Statistics

This endpoint retrieves the statistics for a specific user with the ID 652.

#### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

#### Response Body

- `user_id` (integer): The ID of the user.
    
- `total_logins` (integer): The total number of logins for the user.
    
- `average_session_duration` (string): The average duration of user sessions.
    

Example:

``` json
{
    "user_id": 652,
    "total_logins": 100,
    "average_session_duration": "00:30:00"
}

 ```
